title: Add GT genotype field to VCF
description: Emit FORMAT/GT alongside support fields. Note GT is a formality for subclonal/low-VAF somatic fusions (not diploid germline); AF/DP carry the real signal. Use 0/1 when supported, 0/0 otherwise.
close_reason: Completed: FORMAT/GT emitted first in the genotype column as 0/1 when support exists, 0/0 otherwise. Documented in the VCF header and README as nominal only, since low-frequency somatic fusions are not diploid states and AF/AD carry the real signal.

title: Add breakpoint depth and allele fraction to VCF
description: Low-frequency fusion detection needs a denominator: SR alone is uninterpretable. After clustering, re-query each consensus breakpoint to count spanning reads and emit FORMAT/DP (depth), FORMAT/AD (ref,alt) and AF (allele fraction) per sample. Targeted re-query keeps the streaming/low-memory design since SV counts are small.
close_reason: Completed: after clustering, each consensus breakpoint is re-queried per sample to count spanning reads (by name, skipping supplementary, applying the same duplicate and --min-mapq filters). Emits INFO/DP + INFO/AF cohort-wide and FORMAT/DP:AD:AF per sample, giving the denominator that low-frequency calls need. Verified by an integration test: 1 supporting read against 3 background spanning reads yields DP=4, AF=0.250000, AD=3,1.

title: Support multi-BAM directory input
description: Add CLI and pipeline support for processing multiple BAM files in one run, including shell-expanded inputs such as *.bam or a directory of BAMs. Validate each BAM has a .bai or .csi index, preserve per-BAM/sample provenance in TSV and FASTA output, and define output naming/aggregation behavior for multi-sample runs.
close_reason: Implemented multi-BAM/directory input. --bam now accepts multiple values and directories (shell globs expand to multiple values; directories are scanned non-recursively for *.bam). Each resolved BAM is validated for a .bai/.csi index; inputs are sorted and deduplicated; colliding sample names are rejected. Per-sample provenance added as a trailing 'sample' TSV column and 'sample=' FASTA header field (sample name = BAM file stem). All samples aggregate into the single --output-tsv/--output-fasta. Covered by new unit tests (resolve_bam_inputs, sample_name, has_bam_extension, check_unique_sample_names) and an end-to-end directory integration test. README + CLI help updated. cargo test/clippy -D warnings/fmt all clean.

title: Fix breakpoint exon/intron annotation semantics
description: Correct Stellerator breakpoint region labels to avoid gene-level merged exon numbering and naive breakpoint placement; use transcript-aware exon ordering and more defensible breakpoint-side estimation.

title: Annotate breakpoint regions from GTF/GFF
description: Estimate per-read breakpoint locations using gene models from the annotation file and add columns such as exon3 or intron1 for the query and partner sides of each candidate fusion-supporting read.

title: Support direct second-gene filtering
description: Allow Stellerator users to provide a second target gene and restrict output to reads whose primary and supplementary evidence connect the requested gene intervals.

title: Add integration fixtures for BAM fusion extraction
description: Add end-to-end tests with small indexed BAM fixtures and matching GTF/GFF inputs to validate target interval lookup, SA-tag classification, TSV output, and gzipped FASTA export for the Stellerator prototype.
close_reason: Completed: end-to-end integration test added in tests/fusion_fixture.rs (commit 5c6df1e) builds an indexed BAM + GFF3 and asserts target-interval lookup, SA-tag classification, TSV output, and gzipped FASTA export. Passing.

title: Bootstrap fusion extraction CLI
description: Initialize the Stellerator CLI architecture for extracting fusion-supporting reads from BAM files for user-specified genes using BAM plus GFF/GTF inputs, with compressed FASTA export, concurrent low-memory processing, modular source layout, and debug logging support.

title: Validate and retune --sv-slop default for long reads
description: The 10bp default was a guess, never validated. Long-read breakpoints scatter more in indel-rich/homopolymer contexts, and with only 2-3 supporting reads a too-tight window fragments scarce evidence into singleton calls, potentially sinking real low-frequency events. Validate against real ONT/PacBio data and retune.
notes: Decision (2026-07-20): keep the 10 bp default for now. Geonic will run tests against real data first and retune afterwards, so this stays open pending empirical measurement rather than being changed speculatively. When that data exists, the question to answer is how far supporting-read breakpoints actually scatter around a known junction on ONT/PacBio, and whether the query and partner sides scatter differently enough to warrant separate tolerances.

title: Add --min-mapq filter with permissive default
description: No MAPQ filtering exists; mapping_quality is recorded but never used. Low-MAPQ multi-mapping reads over repeats/paralogs are a dominant false-positive source at low VAF. Add --min-mapq defaulting to 0 (take everything) and warn at startup that all alignments are being taken and may include low-quality reads.
close_reason: Completed: --min-mapq added, defaulting to 0 (take every alignment) with a startup warning that all alignments are being taken and may include low-quality/multi-mapping reads. A floor above 0 also drops records with no reported MAPQ, since their quality cannot be verified. Covered by an integration test asserting both reads pass by default, the warning reaches stderr, and --min-mapq 20 drops the MAPQ-5 read.

title: Derive default output paths from BAM basename and genes
description: When --output-tsv/--output-fasta/--output-vcf are not given, construct a default filename from the input BAM basename and the requested genes/partner (e.g. sample.BCR_ABL1.tsv) instead of the static stellerator.tsv. For multi-BAM/directory runs, use the shared parent directory name. Keep VCF opt-in: skip when the flag is absent, derive a name when the flag is given without a value.
close_reason: Completed: --output-tsv/--output-fasta/--output-vcf now derive <bam-basename>.<genes>.<ext> when a path is not supplied. BAM token is the file stem for a single BAM, the shared parent directory name for several, falling back to the first sample. Gene token joins --gene values with _ and appends a distinct --partner-gene. VCF stays opt-in: absent = skipped, bare flag = derived path, explicit value = that path. Covered by 4 unit tests and 2 integration tests; README documents the scheme. cargo test/clippy -D warnings/fmt all clean.

title: Add discordant-pair and duplicate-aware fusion heuristics
description: Remaining dca sub-items not covered by the SA/partner/VCF work. (1) Use mate placement to add discordant read-pair evidence beyond split-read SA tags. (2) Suppress reads flagged as PCR/optical duplicates in classify_record (currently only unmapped/secondary are skipped). The consensus VCF already de-duplicates distinct reads per junction cluster, but duplicate-flagged reads are still counted as support.
close_reason: Completed both sub-items. (1) Duplicate-flagged reads are skipped alongside unmapped/secondary, with --include-duplicates to opt back in; they no longer inflate TSV rows or VCF support counts. (2) --include-discordant adds mate-placement-derived candidates for reads without an SA tag; evidence class is exposed as a TSV column and FASTA field, and the VCF reports INFO/SR + INFO/PE with FORMAT/SR:PE per sample. Covered by 1 unit test and 2 integration tests; README updated. cargo test (39), clippy -D warnings, fmt all clean.

title: Review current changes and bump version
description: Inspect current repository changes, bump crate version, and run quality checks.

title: Choose longest transcript for breakpoint exon/intron labels
description: Update breakpoint annotation so each called gene uses a single longest transcript model for exon/intron labeling, and add TSV/FASTA output fields for the transcript IDs used for query and partner breakpoint labels.

title: Add bash runner for real Stellerator test inputs
description: Add a repository script that wraps cargo run for the current CLI, validates BAM/index and annotation paths, accepts one or more --gene values plus optional --partner-gene, and writes outputs to a chosen directory so the tool can be exercised against real test data outside Rust unit tests.

title: Improve fusion heuristics and partner annotation
description: Extend the prototype beyond first-SA-tag evidence to better handle multiple supplementary alignments, discordant pairs, partner gene annotation, and duplicate suppression while preserving low-memory streaming behavior.
notes: Delivered the SA/partner/VCF slice: (1) parse_sa_entries now yields every SA-tag alignment (one candidate per supplementary alignment) instead of only the first; (2) partner annotation uses an SA-CIGAR-derived partner breakpoint position for exon/intron and transcript labels; (3) new src/vcf.rs clusters supporting reads into consensus structural variants and writes a multi-sample VCF (4.2) via opt-in --output-vcf/--sv-slop, with BND records carrying gene annotations (GENE1/GENE2), transcripts, breakpoint REGION labels, total support (INFO/SR) and per-sample support (FORMAT/SR). Covered by unit tests (parse_sa_entries, estimate_sa_breakpoint_position, vcf clustering/median) and an end-to-end VCF integration test. Remaining original sub-items (discordant pairs, duplicate-flag suppression) split into Stellerator-kaq.
close_reason: SA-tag-all parsing, improved partner-breakpoint annotation, and consensus-SV VCF output delivered and tested. Remaining heuristics (discordant pairs, duplicate-flag suppression) tracked in Stellerator-kaq.

title: Refresh contributor guide
description: Replace the repository contributor guide with a concise AGENTS.md tailored to the current Rust crate layout and bd workflow.
